# TOKEN_EFFICIENT_AUTHORITY_NAVIGATION_OVERLAY_START

## 0G. Token-efficient authority navigation and implementation-detail preservation overlay

document status: active additive navigation hardening supplement

This overlay improves token efficiency without deleting, replacing, shrinking, summarizing, moving, or weakening any existing TRADER_1 baseline content, automatic profit-convergence reinforcement content, profitability-convergence optimizer content, schema content, validator content, promotion threshold, live blocker, retained archive, or safety rule. The source document remains preserved below this overlay and remains available for exact-detail implementation, traceability, coverage, validation, and retained-archive lookup.

```yaml
overlay_schema_id: trader1.token_efficient_authority_navigation_overlay.v1
patch_class: DOC_CONTRACT_PATCH
patch_date: "2026-04-28T09:12:27Z"
patch_method: additive_navigation_overlay_plus_verbatim_baseline_preservation
patch_input_trader1_file: "TRADER_1(150).md"
patch_input_trader1_sha256: "5c8e61807cf8ade9797f7ec5a4c2615a965d314586eaf26278a16f9f32c7a31b"
patch_input_agents_file: "AGENTS(158).md"
patch_input_agents_sha256: "669f3b1cc753f63435be38b23af16dcc2b75a7bf7f3266259f5aac3af23245ba"
file_split: false
removed_requirements: []
detail_reduction_allowed: false
semantic_reduction_allowed: false
retained_archive_preserved: true
baseline_body_preserved_verbatim: true
existing_detail_preserved: true
source_replacement_created: false
summary_as_replacement_allowed: false
token_optimization_method: read_routing_indexing_context_cache
profit_guarantee_created: false
live_order_ready_after_document_patch: false
live_order_allowed_after_document_patch: false
can_live_trade_after_document_patch: false
```

### 0G.1 Purpose and invariant

The purpose of this overlay is to reduce repeated AI token consumption while preserving full implementation precision.

The method is not compression by requirement removal. The method is authority navigation.

```text
token efficiency -> read routing
token efficiency -> section and requirement addressing
token efficiency -> generated read caches
token efficiency -> context packs with source hashes
token efficiency -> schema and validator expansion on demand
token efficiency -> retained archive default-read=false
```

Forbidden outcomes:

```text
token optimization -> requirement deletion
token optimization -> requirement summary as replacement
token optimization -> schema detail loss
token optimization -> validator detail loss
token optimization -> live safety weakening
token optimization -> promotion threshold weakening
token optimization -> optimizer or convergence detail weakening
token optimization -> retained archive removal
```

The default live state remains fixed.

```text
live_order_ready=false
live_order_allowed=false
can_live_trade=false
```

### 0G.2 Active read surface

AI compiler and implementation agents must use the following active read surface before expanding the full document.

```text
ACTIVE_READ_SURFACE_START

MUST_READ_DEFAULT:
  TRADER_1.0G token-efficient authority navigation overlay
  TRADER_1.0F automatic profit-convergence reinforcement overlay
  TRADER_1.0E profitability-convergence optimizer overlay
  TRADER_1.0D lossless validated improvement overlay
  TRADER_1 active contract pack
  TRADER_1 live safety, LIVE_READY writer guard, and live final guard sections
  AGENTS active implementation guide and matching navigation overlay
  current task class section from read_routing_table
  current MVP section
  current patch_result contract fields

READ_IF_REFERENCED:
  exact schema blocks
  exact validator definitions
  exact convergence layer details
  exact optimizer topic details
  exact exchange adapter details
  exact dashboard details
  exact test fixture matrix
  exact retained archive coverage entry

READ_FOR_VALIDATION_ONLY:
  retained archive
  historical patch source
  duplicate preserved baseline
  source preservation blocks
  document normalization archive

READ_FOR_CONFLICT_ONLY:
  lower-authority repeated wording
  older schema examples
  historical supplement duplicates
  retained archive wording that conflicts with active authority

ACTIVE_READ_SURFACE_END
```

Rules:

```text
Default read scope is not the full document.
Default non-read scope is not deleted.
When exact implementation detail is needed, expand the exact source section by section_id or requirement_id.
If generated read cache conflicts with this source document, this source document wins.
If active read surface conflicts with live safety, live safety wins.
```

Every generated navigation artifact must address important source regions with stable section IDs. Section IDs are addressing metadata, not replacement text.

Minimum section IDs:

### 0G.4 Requirement ID system

Generated requirement indexes must map fine-grained implementation requirements without replacing the source text.

Required requirement ID prefixes:

```text
REQ-AUTH
REQ-REG
REQ-SCHEMA
REQ-VAL
REQ-PATCH
REQ-MVP
REQ-NS
REQ-TRUTH
REQ-DECISION
REQ-ORDER
REQ-LIVE
REQ-LIVE-WRITER
REQ-TEST
REQ-DASH
REQ-SEC
REQ-OPT
REQ-CONV
REQ-RISK
REQ-ADAPTER
REQ-ARCHIVE
```

Minimum required requirement IDs for optimizer and convergence implementation:

```text
REQ-OPT-001 optimizer is not profit guarantee
REQ-OPT-002 optimizer cannot create live permission
REQ-OPT-003 optimizer output cannot write LIVE_READY snapshot
REQ-OPT-004 cost-adjusted objective required
REQ-OPT-005 candidate scorecard required
REQ-OPT-006 candidate ranking required
REQ-OPT-007 exploration to exploitation transition required
REQ-OPT-008 adaptive parameter narrowing required
REQ-OPT-009 symbol strategy regime fit required
REQ-OPT-010 execution feedback loop required
REQ-OPT-011 overfit diagnostic required
REQ-OPT-012 convergence claim validator required
REQ-OPT-013 optimizer guardrail required

REQ-CONV-001 convergence system is not profit guarantee
REQ-CONV-002 stateful memory layer required
REQ-CONV-003 objective hierarchy required
REQ-CONV-004 controller state machine required
REQ-CONV-005 market regime adaptation required
REQ-CONV-006 failure root cause learning required
REQ-CONV-007 overfit and robustness defense required
REQ-CONV-008 execution calibration required
REQ-CONV-009 adaptive risk scaling gated by validators
REQ-CONV-010 survival layer has priority over profitability
REQ-CONV-011 convergence assessment claims are scoped
REQ-CONV-012 scale-up requires burn-in and validators
REQ-CONV-013 convergence artifacts cannot create live permission
```

Each requirement index entry must include:

```text
requirement_id
source_section_id
source_file
source_heading
full_text_marker
authority_level
requirement_title
requirement_kind
schema_ids
validator_ids
artifact_ids
test_ids
mvp_stage
implementation_depth_min
blocking_level
live_affecting
read_when
depends_on
conflicts_resolve_by
source_text_sha256
implementation_status
test_status
```

### 0G.5 Read routing table

AI compiler must classify work by task class before source expansion.

```yaml
read_routing_table:
  MVP0_CONTRACT_BASELINE:
    required_sections:
      - SECTION_AUTHORITY_ORDER
      - SECTION_ACTIVE_CONTRACT_PACK
      - SECTION_REGISTRY_SEED
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS
      - SECTION_VALIDATOR_REGISTRY
      - SECTION_PATCH_RESULT
      - SECTION_MVP_LADDER
    expand_only_if_needed:
      - SECTION_RETAINED_ARCHIVE
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_COMMON_DEFS
    default_forbidden_full_scan:
      - SECTION_RETAINED_ARCHIVE

  SCHEMA_GENERATION:
    required_sections:
      - SECTION_REGISTRY_SEED
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_COMMON_DEFS
      - SECTION_PATCH_RESULT
    expand_only_if_needed:
      - exact schema block by schema_id
      - exact enum source by enum_ref
    default_forbidden_full_scan:
      - SECTION_RETAINED_ARCHIVE

  VALIDATOR_IMPLEMENTATION:
    required_sections:
      - SECTION_VALIDATOR_REGISTRY
      - SECTION_VALIDATOR_FIXTURES
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS
      - SECTION_PATCH_RESULT
    expand_only_if_needed:
      - exact validator dependencies
      - exact fixture case
      - exact schema input and output
    default_forbidden_full_scan:
      - SECTION_RETAINED_ARCHIVE

  PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_MVP0:

      - SECTION_PATCH_RESULT
      - SECTION_REGISTRY_SEED
      - SECTION_SCHEMA_CONTRACTS

  PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_MVP3:
```

### 0G.6 Progressive disclosure levels

Source content must be read by progressive disclosure.

```text
MUST_READ:
  active overlays
  active contract pack
  live safety gates
  current task class sections
  current MVP stage

READ_IF_REFERENCED:
  exact schema definitions
  exact validator definitions
  exact optimizer and convergence slices
  exact exchange adapter details
  exact dashboard details

READ_FOR_VALIDATION_ONLY:
  retained archive
  historical patch source
  requirement preservation source blocks
  duplicate preserved baseline

READ_FOR_CONFLICT_ONLY:
  lower authority repeated wording
  archive wording that appears to conflict with active authority
  old schema examples
```

Progressive disclosure must not be used to skip required implementation detail. When a task requires exact field names, enum values, validator dependencies, or fixture cases, the exact source section must be expanded.

### 0G.7 Retained archive default-read=false policy

The retained archive is preserved, but it is not default reading material.

```text
retained_archive_default_read=false
retained_archive_authority=ARCHIVE_ONLY_NON_AUTHORITY_TRACEABILITY_ONLY
retained_archive_not_deleted=true
retained_archive_requirements_not_removed=true
```

Retained archive may be read only when:

```text
coverage_index lookup requires it
active section references a coverage_id
requirement preservation validation requires it
contract_gap source traceability requires it
omitted-detail lookup is required and no active source resolves the issue
user explicitly requests full retained archive review
```

Full retained archive scan is forbidden by default during ordinary implementation.

### 0G.8 Generated navigation artifacts

MVP-0 or the first document-navigation contract patch must require these generated artifacts.

```text
contracts/generated/authority_section_map.json
contracts/generated/requirement_index.json
contracts/generated/requirement_artifact_matrix.json
contracts/generated/read_cache_manifest.json
contracts/generated/ACTIVE_WORKING_VIEW.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/MVP0_CONTRACT_BASELINE.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/SCHEMA_GENERATION.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/VALIDATOR_IMPLEMENTATION.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_MVP0.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_MVP3.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/OPTIMIZER_MVP3.md
contracts/generated/context_pack/LIVE_BLOCKED_TEST.md
contracts/generated/current_implementation_state.json
system/evidence/implementation_patch_ledger.json
```

Rules:

```text
generated navigation artifact is not authority
generated navigation artifact is read cache
generated navigation artifact cannot replace source requirements
generated navigation artifact must include source section IDs and source hashes
generated navigation artifact conflict with TRADER_1 -> TRADER_1 wins
generated navigation artifact conflict with AGENTS -> TRADER_1 wins first, then AGENTS if non-conflicting
```

### 0G.9 Read cache invalidation

Generated read caches are valid only while their source hashes match.

Required `read_cache_manifest` fields:

```text
manifest_schema_id
created_at_utc
trader1_sha256
agents_sha256
authority_section_map_sha256
requirement_index_sha256
requirement_artifact_matrix_sha256
registry_yaml_sha256_when_generated
schema_bundle_sha256_when_generated
context_pack_hashes
active_working_view_sha256
current_implementation_state_sha256
valid_until_authority_hash_changes
```

Invalidation rules:

```text
TRADER_1 hash changed -> regenerate all navigation artifacts
AGENTS hash changed -> regenerate all navigation artifacts
section hash changed -> regenerate affected context pack
requirement index changed -> regenerate affected context pack
registry hash changed -> regenerate registry-dependent context packs
schema bundle hash changed -> regenerate schema-dependent context packs
implementation state stale -> regenerate from patch ledger and validator results
```

### 0G.10 Schema expansion on demand

Schema full expansion must be on demand.

Read schema summary by default:

```text
schema_id
required fields
linked requirement_ids
linked validator_ids
linked artifact_ids
blocking conditions
```

Read full schema only when:

```text
generating schema file
implementing schema validator
validating exact schema field
resolving schema conflict
generating registry projection
validating LIVE_READY writer input
validating patch_result
```

This rule does not remove schema detail. It controls when exact schema detail is expanded.

### 0G.11 Common definition reference policy

Common definitions must be referenced after their first authoritative definition.

Required reference names:

```text
ENUM:no_trade_reason
ENUM:live_blocker_code
ENUM:validator_result_status
ENUM:order_failure_type
ENUM:patch_class
ENUM:mode
ENUM:exchange
ENUM:market_type
ENUM:risk_profile
DEF:blocker_object
DEF:validator_result
DEF:patch_result
DEF:evidence_manifest
DEF:live_ready_snapshot
DEF:optimizer_scorecard
DEF:optimizer_guardrail
DEF:convergence_assessment
DEF:risk_scaling_decision
```

Rules:

```text
first authoritative definition remains preserved
reference does not delete individual enum values
generated schemas may use shared $defs or registry projection
exact expansion is required when generating concrete schema output
```

Validator group references may be used for navigation and patch planning. They do not delete individual validator definitions.

### 0G.13 Optimizer and convergence topic slicing

Optimizer and convergence content must be addressable by topic slices.

Optimizer slices:

```text
OPT_SLICE_CONFIG
OPT_SLICE_OBJECTIVE
OPT_SLICE_SCORECARD
OPT_SLICE_RANKING
OPT_SLICE_EXPLOITATION
OPT_SLICE_NARROWING
OPT_SLICE_SYMBOL_REGIME_FIT
OPT_SLICE_EXECUTION_FEEDBACK
OPT_SLICE_OVERFIT
OPT_SLICE_CONVERGENCE_CLAIM
OPT_SLICE_GUARDRAIL
OPT_SLICE_DASHBOARD
```

Convergence slices:

```text
CONV_SLICE_OBJECTIVE
CONV_SLICE_MEMORY
CONV_SLICE_CONTROLLER
CONV_SLICE_REGIME_ADAPTATION
CONV_SLICE_FAILURE_LEARNING
CONV_SLICE_OVERFIT_DEFENSE
CONV_SLICE_EXECUTION_CALIBRATION
CONV_SLICE_RISK_SCALING
CONV_SLICE_SURVIVAL
CONV_SLICE_ASSESSMENT
CONV_SLICE_DASHBOARD
```

Each slice must map to:

```text
required_schema_ids
required_validator_ids
required_artifact_ids
required_fixture_ids
required_patch_result_fields
blocking_codes
live_safety_dependencies
implementation_depth_min
```

### 0G.14 Implementation depth levels

Token-efficient reading must not cause shallow implementation.

Closed implementation depth levels:

```text
DEPTH_0_SCAFFOLD_ONLY
DEPTH_1_SCHEMA_AND_ARTIFACT_STRUCTURE
DEPTH_2_VALIDATOR_LOGIC
DEPTH_3_NEGATIVE_FIXTURES
DEPTH_4_RUNTIME_INTEGRATION
DEPTH_5_EVIDENCE_AND_STAGE_GATE
DEPTH_6_DASHBOARD_AND_OPERATOR_VISIBILITY
```

Minimum depth defaults:

```text
MVP0 registry and schema -> DEPTH_1_SCHEMA_AND_ARTIFACT_STRUCTURE
live-blocking validator -> DEPTH_3_NEGATIVE_FIXTURES
optimizer ranking path -> DEPTH_4_RUNTIME_INTEGRATION
convergence memory path -> DEPTH_4_RUNTIME_INTEGRATION
convergence risk scaling -> DEPTH_5_EVIDENCE_AND_STAGE_GATE
dashboard readiness display -> DEPTH_6_DASHBOARD_AND_OPERATOR_VISIBILITY
live enabling path -> DEPTH_5_EVIDENCE_AND_STAGE_GATE plus all existing live evidence
```

### 0G.15 Requirement to artifact matrix

`contracts/generated/requirement_artifact_matrix.json` must map each requirement to concrete implementation outputs.

Required fields:

```text
requirement_id
section_id
schema_files
validator_files
test_files
fixture_files
runtime_modules
evidence_artifacts
dashboard_artifacts
patch_result_fields
minimum_depth
live_affecting
status
```

If a requirement is live-affecting and has no mapped validator or test, live_order_allowed=false for the affected scope.

### 0G.16 Task-specific acceptance checklists

Generated context packs must include acceptance checklists. These checklists are not replacements for source requirements; they are task-local verification aids.

Minimum checklist topics:

```text
MVP0_CONTRACT_BASELINE_ACCEPTANCE
SCHEMA_GENERATION_ACCEPTANCE
VALIDATOR_IMPLEMENTATION_ACCEPTANCE
OPTIMIZER_RANKING_ACCEPTANCE
CONVERGENCE_MEMORY_ACCEPTANCE
CONVERGENCE_RISK_SCALING_ACCEPTANCE
LIVE_BLOCKED_TEST_ACCEPTANCE
DASHBOARD_UX_ACCEPTANCE
BUNDLE_SECURITY_ACCEPTANCE
```

Checklist rule:

```text
acceptance checklist cannot weaken source requirement
acceptance checklist cannot omit linked requirement_id
acceptance checklist failure -> affected path BLOCKED or UNTESTED
```

### 0G.17 Future overlay delta-only rule

Future overlays must be delta-oriented to prevent uncontrolled document growth.

```text
future_overlay_delta_only=true
```

Rules:

```text
Do not restate a complete existing live gate unless changing it.
Do not restate a complete existing validator list unless changing it.
Do not restate a complete existing enum list unless changing it.
Reference existing requirement_id and section_id.
State strengthening delta only.
State unchanged preserved requirements.
State no live permission created.
State removed_requirements=[].
```

If a future overlay repeats an existing rule for safety emphasis, it must mark the repeated text as `reaffirmation_only`, not as a new conflicting authority.

### 0G.18 Duplicate preservation marker

Duplicate preserved blocks must be marked or indexed as non-authority duplicates.

```text
DUPLICATE_PRESERVATION_BLOCK:
  authority_status: NON_AUTHORITY_DUPLICATE
  default_read: false
  use_for: traceability coverage omitted_detail_lookup
  can_weaken_active: false
  can_create_permission: false
  can_create_readiness: false
```

Repeated preserved wording cannot create a looser exception.

### 0G.19 Exact expansion trigger

Full source expansion is allowed only when one of these triggers exists.

```text
authority_section_map missing
authority_section_map stale
read_cache_manifest stale
authority hash changed
section hash mismatch
contract conflict detected
schema extraction required
validator implementation requires exact fields
requirement preservation validation required
coverage validation required
retained archive coverage lookup required
user explicitly requests full document review
```

When exact expansion occurs, the patch result must record:

```text
expanded_section_ids
expansion_reason
source_hash_checked
retained_archive_read
full_document_read
```

### 0G.20 Patch result extension for navigation

Every token-navigation or generated-read-cache patch must include:

```text
token_navigation_patch
active_read_surface_used
task_class
required_section_ids
expanded_section_ids
forbidden_default_sections_respected
authority_section_map_status
requirement_index_status
requirement_artifact_matrix_status
read_cache_manifest_status
context_pack_status
current_implementation_state_status
retained_archive_read
full_document_read
read_cache_invalidated
next_task_class
next_required_section_ids
next_optional_section_ids
next_forbidden_default_sections
```

Required values:

```text
removed_requirements=[]
file_split=false
detail_reduction_allowed=false
semantic_reduction_allowed=false
non-LIVE_ENABLING_PATCH -> live_order_allowed_after=false
```

### 0G.21 Acceptance condition for token optimization patches

A token optimization patch is valid only if all conditions hold.

```text
removed_requirements=[]
file_split=false
detail_reduction_allowed=false
semantic_reduction_allowed=false
baseline_body_preserved_verbatim=true
retained_archive_preserved=true
active_requirements_preserved=true
all new section ids mapped
all new requirement ids mapped
coverage_index_update_required=true
no live permission created
live_order_ready_after=false unless independent LIVE_ENABLING evidence exists
live_order_allowed_after=false unless independent LIVE_ENABLING evidence exists
```

### 0G.22 Final token-efficient operating rule

```text
The full document remains the authority. The AI compiler should not spend tokens rereading the full document by default. It must use section IDs, requirement IDs, read routing, generated read caches, context packs, validator groups, schema references, and exact expansion triggers to read less while implementing more precisely. Token efficiency is valid only when implementation detail, validator coverage, evidence quality, live safety, retained archive preservation, and requirement traceability are not weakened.
```

# TOKEN_EFFICIENT_AUTHORITY_NAVIGATION_OVERLAY_END


# AUTOMATIC_PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_REINFORCEMENT_OVERLAY_START

## 0F. Automatic profit-convergence reinforcement overlay

document status: active additive hardening supplement

This overlay strengthens the existing profitability-convergence optimizer into a stateful, evidence-backed, self-correcting profit-convergence system. It does not delete, replace, shrink, summarize, move, or weaken any existing TRADER_1 baseline content, optimizer overlay content, schema content, retained archive content, validator content, promotion threshold, live blocker, or safety rule. The existing document remains preserved verbatim below this overlay and remains available for implementation detail, traceability, coverage, and retained-archive lookup.

```yaml
overlay_schema_id: trader1.automatic_profit_convergence_reinforcement_overlay.v1
patch_class: DOC_CONTRACT_PATCH
patch_date: "2026-04-28T07:30:00Z"
patch_method: additive_overlay_plus_verbatim_baseline_preservation
patch_input_trader1_file: "TRADER_1(137).md"
patch_input_trader1_sha256: "8c3f5901d7acbb5dbacfcea2dfdcc19da3be7c0a5eacfc95957aaf41a38aab8a"
patch_input_agents_file: "AGENTS(145).md"
patch_input_agents_sha256: "7fd23928215f62919407b69d8512b498479064af139148c85dbf69f864a34994"
file_split: false
removed_requirements: []
detail_reduction_allowed: false
semantic_reduction_allowed: false
retained_archive_preserved: true
baseline_body_preserved_verbatim: true
existing_detail_preserved: true
profit_guarantee_created: false
live_order_ready_after_document_patch: false
live_order_allowed_after_document_patch: false
can_live_trade_after_document_patch: false
```

### 0F.1 Purpose and non-goals

This reinforcement makes the optimizer explicitly stateful and self-correcting. The target behavior is not a one-shot strategy search. The target behavior is a repeated closed-loop system that learns from replay, paper, shadow, read-only live observation, limited live burn-in evidence when allowed, failed candidates, execution defects, market-regime shifts, and risk events.

The reinforced system must optimize for durable net expected value after all costs and penalties, not for raw PnL, isolated backtest wins, single-symbol wins, or short-term lucky outcomes.

The reinforcement is not a profit guarantee.

```text
convergence system exists -> no profitability claim
convergence score improves -> no profitability claim
stable candidate detected -> no live permission
writer-input eligible -> no LIVE_READY snapshot
limited live burn-in success -> no automatic scale-up without validator gates
risk scaling recommendation -> no direct position size mutation
memory says candidate is strong -> no live config mutation
```

The default live state remains fixed.

```text
live_order_ready=false
live_order_allowed=false
can_live_trade=false
```

### 0F.2 Reinforced convergence architecture

The profit-convergence system is composed of the following required layers.

```text
1. cost-adjusted objective layer
2. long-term learning memory layer
3. exploration-to-exploitation controller
4. market-regime adaptation layer
5. failure root-cause learning layer
6. overfit and robustness defense layer
7. execution feedback calibration layer
8. adaptive risk-scaling layer
9. survival and drawdown protection layer
10. convergence assessment and claim-control layer
```

Each layer must produce artifacts, must be validator-addressable, and must remain namespace-scoped by exchange, market_type, mode, session_id, strategy_id, strategy_build_id, parameter_hash, timeframe_scope, and regime_scope when applicable.

No layer can bypass FinalDecision, risk veto, ledger, reconciliation, official API verification, emergency protection, live final guard, LIVE_READY snapshot writer, or live_order_allowed.

### 0F.3 Reinforced objective hierarchy

The system must optimize a constrained objective rather than a raw score.

Default objective shape:

```text
convergence_objective_score =
    weighted_net_ev_after_cost_score
  - drawdown_penalty
  - tail_loss_penalty
  - downside_volatility_penalty
  - slippage_error_penalty
  - impact_error_penalty
  - fee_error_penalty
  - latency_error_penalty
  - overfit_penalty
  - regime_fragility_penalty
  - symbol_concentration_penalty
  - liquidity_fragility_penalty
  - operational_risk_penalty
  - resource_cost_penalty
  - model_drift_penalty
```

Required objective rules:

```text
raw_pnl_score alone is invalid
net_ev_after_cost is mandatory
fee, spread, slippage, impact, latency, and funding when applicable are mandatory cost components
missing cost component -> OBJECTIVE_FUNCTION_MISSING or COST_AFTER_EDGE_UNVERIFIED
negative net_ev_after_cost -> candidate cannot be promoted
high drawdown with high net_ev -> candidate remains blocked from LIVE_READY input
unstable objective across windows -> convergence status cannot be ROBUSTLY_IMPROVING
objective formula changes -> REGISTRY_PATCH + SCHEMA_PATCH + VALIDATOR_PATCH + evidence update
objective weights must be registry-backed and evidence-versioned
```

The objective must be evaluated across at least these dimensions when evidence exists.

```text
short rolling window
medium rolling window
long rolling window
regime window
symbol window
adapter execution window
paper/live parity window when available
live burn-in window when live evidence exists
```

### 0F.4 Long-term learning memory layer

The optimizer must maintain a durable memory of what has been tried, what failed, what improved, what was rejected, and why. This memory must not become execution truth and must not create live permission.

Required memory entities:

```text
strategy_performance_memory
parameter_outcome_memory
symbol_regime_fit_memory
failure_pattern_memory
execution_calibration_memory
candidate_lineage_memory
objective_weight_version_memory
risk_scaling_memory
convergence_state_memory
```

Memory rules:

```text
memory artifact cannot place orders
memory artifact cannot mutate live config
memory artifact cannot write LIVE_READY snapshot
memory artifact cannot become ACTIVE snapshot
memory artifact cannot override validator FAIL
memory artifact cannot override risk veto
memory artifact cannot override official API verification failure
memory artifact cannot erase failed candidates without audit
memory artifact cannot silently forget retired or blocked candidates
```

Every memory update must record:

```text
memory_record_id
created_at_utc
updated_at_utc
exchange
market_type
mode
strategy_id
strategy_build_id
parameter_hash
regime_scope
timeframe_scope
source_evidence_ids
source_validator_result_ids
previous_memory_hash
memory_hash
update_reason
live_permission_created=false
```

### 0F.5 Exploration-to-exploitation controller

The optimizer must dynamically choose between broad exploration, local exploitation, robustness testing, parameter narrowing, and retirement. The transition must be evidence-backed and reversible.

Controller states:

```text
DISCOVERY
BROAD_EXPLORATION
LOCAL_EXPLOITATION
ROBUSTNESS_SWEEP
PARAMETER_NARROWING
PAPER_CONFIRMATION
SHADOW_CONFIRMATION
WRITER_INPUT_REVIEW
LIVE_BURN_IN_OBSERVATION
SCALE_UP_REVIEW
DEGRADED_REVIEW
RETIREMENT_REVIEW
BLOCKED
```

Transition rules:

```text
DISCOVERY -> BROAD_EXPLORATION requires valid search_space_snapshot
BROAD_EXPLORATION -> LOCAL_EXPLOITATION requires ranking_stability_validator PASS
LOCAL_EXPLOITATION -> ROBUSTNESS_SWEEP requires local improvement plus resource budget PASS
ROBUSTNESS_SWEEP -> PARAMETER_NARROWING requires overfit diagnostic not HIGH and robustness evidence PASS
PARAMETER_NARROWING -> PAPER_CONFIRMATION requires rollback plan and holdout/OOS/walk-forward evidence
PAPER_CONFIRMATION -> WRITER_INPUT_REVIEW requires promotion thresholds PASS
WRITER_INPUT_REVIEW -> LIVE_READY snapshot requires live_ready_snapshot_writer_validator PASS
LIVE_BURN_IN_OBSERVATION -> SCALE_UP_REVIEW requires paper/live parity, execution quality, drawdown, and risk validators PASS
any state -> DEGRADED_REVIEW when drift, drawdown, execution degradation, or data quality degradation is detected
any state -> BLOCKED when live mutation attempt, namespace violation, validator FAIL, HIGH contract_gap, or CRITICAL contract_gap exists
```

Exploration and exploitation controls:

```text
if ranking stability improves and overfit risk remains low -> reduce broad exploration share and increase local exploitation share
if performance diverges or regime changes -> increase exploration share and reduce live expansion eligibility
if resource pressure is HIGH or CRITICAL -> pause exploration before affecting ledger, reconciliation, live guard, dashboard health, or safety processes
if candidate family concentration exceeds limit -> prune or down-rank overrepresented family
if candidate count exceeds budget -> throttle generation and preserve audit trail
```

### 0F.6 Market-regime adaptation layer

The optimizer must not assume that one strategy is universally good. It must learn strategy fitness by regime, symbol, timeframe, liquidity tier, exchange, and market_type.

Required regime-adaptation dimensions:

```text
exchange
market_type
symbol
symbol_liquidity_tier
strategy_id
strategy_build_id
parameter_hash
timeframe_scope
holding_time_estimate
market_regime
regime_family
volatility_bucket
spread_bucket
depth_bucket
trend_quality_bucket
mean_reversion_quality_bucket
risk_profile
```

Rules:

```text
regime fit cannot be inferred from a single indicator
regime-specific winner cannot transfer to another regime without evidence
symbol-specific winner cannot transfer to another symbol without evidence
exchange-specific winner cannot transfer to another exchange without evidence
spot winner cannot transfer to futures without evidence
read-only live observation may improve estimates but cannot create live permission
DOWNTREND spot long remains restricted unless validated exception exists
UNCERTAIN regime blocks aggressive entry and blocks scale-up
```

The layer must support these outputs only:

```text
regime_fit_report
symbol_fit_report
strategy_regime_fit_matrix
regime_shift_alert
candidate_scope_restriction
promotion_blocker
```

It must not output:

```text
live order
live config mutation
LIVE_READY snapshot
ACTIVE snapshot
risk profile upgrade
```

### 0F.7 Failure root-cause learning layer

Every material candidate failure, paper failure, shadow divergence, live burn-in degradation, or optimizer regression must be classified by root cause, not only by final score.

Closed root-cause categories:

```text
ENTRY_TIMING_ERROR
EXIT_TIMING_ERROR
STOP_PLACEMENT_ERROR
TAKE_PROFIT_STRUCTURE_ERROR
REGIME_MISMATCH
SYMBOL_LIQUIDITY_MISMATCH
SPREAD_COST_UNDERESTIMATED
SLIPPAGE_UNDERESTIMATED
MARKET_IMPACT_UNDERESTIMATED
FEE_MODEL_ERROR
LATENCY_SENSITIVITY
PARTIAL_FILL_MODEL_ERROR
ORDER_REJECT_MODEL_ERROR
DATA_STALENESS
DATA_GAP
OVERFIT
OUTLIER_DOMINANCE
SAMPLE_INSUFFICIENT
CONCENTRATION_RISK
RISK_LIMIT_TOO_LOOSE
RESOURCE_PRESSURE
EXCHANGE_RULE_MISMATCH
UNKNOWN_ROOT_CAUSE
```

Failure-learning rules:

```text
loss without root-cause classification -> FAILURE_ANALYSIS_MISSING
repeat failure with same root cause -> candidate family down-ranked or blocked
cost-underestimation failure -> objective penalty increased or candidate blocked
regime-mismatch failure -> candidate scope narrowed or blocked
execution failure -> execution realism and fill-quality validators must update future ranking
unknown root cause in live-affecting path -> live_order_allowed=false for affected scope
```

### 0F.8 Overfit and robustness defense layer

The optimizer must treat overfitting as a blocking safety defect, not as a minor statistical issue.

Required diagnostics:

```text
walk_forward_validation
out_of_sample_validation
bootstrap_stability
permutation_test
parameter_sensitivity
symbol_concentration_analysis
regime_concentration_analysis
outlier_dominance_check
cost_stress_test
slippage_stress_test
impact_stress_test
liquidity_stress_test
timeframe_sensitivity_check
data_quality_audit
survivorship_bias_check
data_snooping_check
multiple_comparison_penalty
```

Blocking rules:

```text
walk_forward missing -> WALK_FORWARD_MISSING
OOS missing -> OOS_MISSING
holdout missing -> HOLDOUT_MISSING
bootstrap unstable -> BOOTSTRAP_UNSTABLE
overfit risk HIGH -> promotion BLOCKED
one outlier dominates profit -> promotion BLOCKED
one symbol dominates profit -> concentration review required
one regime dominates profit -> regime-scoped candidate only
parameter sensitivity extreme -> parameter narrowing BLOCKED
multiple comparison penalty unresolved -> convergence claim BLOCKED
```

### 0F.9 Execution feedback calibration layer

The optimizer must update future scoring from realized execution quality. It must not treat paper execution as live execution.

Required feedback comparisons:

```text
expected_fee_bps vs realized_fee_bps
expected_spread_cost_bps vs realized_spread_cost_bps
expected_slippage_bps vs realized_slippage_bps
expected_impact_bps vs realized_impact_bps
expected_latency_cost_bps vs realized_latency_cost_bps
expected_fill_rate vs realized_fill_rate
expected_partial_fill_rate vs realized_partial_fill_rate
expected_reject_rate vs realized_reject_rate
expected_reconciliation_events vs realized_reconciliation_events
```

Calibration rules:

```text
paper feedback can improve paper model only
read-only live observation can improve estimates but cannot create live permission
limited live burn-in feedback can affect risk-reducing and conservative refinements only until scale-up validators PASS
realized slippage worse than expected -> reduce candidate score or block scale-up
realized impact worse than expected -> reduce symbol liquidity fit or block affected symbol
fee mismatch -> FEE_MODEL_UNVERIFIED
fill quality poor -> EXECUTION_QUALITY_UNTESTED or BLOCKED
ambiguous transport events -> block optimization-driven order changes until reconciliation evidence exists
```

### 0F.10 Adaptive risk-scaling layer

Risk scaling must be slower upward than downward. The optimizer may recommend risk scaling, but it must not directly change live size or risk profile.

Allowed risk-scaling outputs:

```text
risk_scale_down_recommendation
risk_hold_recommendation
risk_scale_up_candidate
risk_profile_downgrade_recommendation
risk_profile_upgrade_candidate
strategy_pause_recommendation
symbol_pause_recommendation
```

Forbidden direct actions:

```text
optimizer directly increases position size
optimizer directly upgrades risk profile
optimizer directly widens stop after losses
optimizer directly relaxes exposure cap
optimizer directly mutates LIVE_READY snapshot
optimizer directly mutates ACTIVE snapshot
```

Scale-up requirements:

```text
live burn-in window PASS
paper/live parity PASS
realized slippage within threshold
realized impact within threshold
fee error within threshold
drawdown within profile threshold
no HIGH contract_gap
no CRITICAL contract_gap
all live-blocking validators PASS
operator policy allows scale-up
risk scaling validator PASS
```

Scale-down triggers:

```text
realized drawdown threshold breach
three consecutive execution quality failures
slippage p95 above threshold
impact error above threshold
fee model mismatch
regime shift invalidates strategy scope
reconciliation defect
ledger integrity defect
resource critical state
operator stop or safe mode
```

Scale-down may be immediate when risk-reducing. Scale-up must be gradual and validator-gated.

### 0F.11 Survival and drawdown protection layer

The system must survive long enough to converge. Survival has higher priority than profitability.

Survival rules:

```text
max_daily_loss breach -> new entries blocked
max_weekly_loss breach -> new entries blocked
max_monthly_loss breach -> strategy or portfolio freeze according to risk profile
max_mdd_freeze breach -> freeze affected live path
ledger integrity failure -> live orders blocked
reconciliation failure -> live orders blocked
emergency protection unavailable -> live_order_ready=false
kill switch unavailable -> live_order_ready=false
resource critical -> live_order_allowed=false for new entries
```

Survival layer outputs:

```text
NO_TRADE
SAFE_MODE
READ_ONLY
RECONCILE_REQUIRED
KILL_SWITCH
TRADE_DISABLED
BLOCKED
```

Survival layer cannot be weakened by optimizer score, candidate rank, convergence status, or paper performance.

### 0F.12 Convergence assessment layer

Convergence claims must be scoped and evidence-backed.

Closed convergence states:

```text
NOT_STARTED
COLLECTING
DIVERGING
NOISY
LOCALLY_IMPROVING
ROBUSTLY_IMPROVING
STABLE_CANDIDATE
WRITER_INPUT_ELIGIBLE
LIVE_BURN_IN_OBSERVING
SCALE_UP_ELIGIBLE
DEGRADED
RETIRED
BLOCKED
```

Minimum requirements for LOCALLY_IMPROVING:

```text
short_window net_ev_after_cost non-negative or improving
candidate score stable across at least two cycles
no hard blocker for affected research path
sample not completely insufficient
```

Minimum requirements for ROBUSTLY_IMPROVING:

```text
medium_window positive net_ev_after_cost or explicit low-frequency blocker
walk-forward PASS
OOS PASS
bootstrap stability PASS
execution quality not UNTESTED
sample confidence PASS
risk profile drawdown within threshold
no HIGH contract_gap
no CRITICAL contract_gap
no blocking validator FAIL
```

Minimum requirements for STABLE_CANDIDATE:

```text
ROBUSTLY_IMPROVING
regime robustness PASS or scope restriction recorded
symbol robustness PASS or scope restriction recorded
cost stress PASS
slippage stress PASS
liquidity stress PASS
outlier dominance PASS
failure root-cause review complete
```

Minimum requirements for WRITER_INPUT_ELIGIBLE:

```text
STABLE_CANDIDATE
all promotion thresholds PASS
paper_live_parity PASS when applicable
execution feedback PASS when applicable
cost_after_edge PASS
live_ready_snapshot_writer_validator not yet used to create snapshot unless separately executed
```

Minimum requirements for SCALE_UP_ELIGIBLE:

```text
limited live burn-in PASS
live burn-in window satisfied
paper/live parity still PASS
realized execution quality PASS
risk scaling validator PASS
operator policy allows scale-up
no live safety blocker
```

Forbidden standalone convergence claims:

```text
profitable
profit converged
optimizer solved
self-optimizing profit
automatic profit
guaranteed profit
```

Allowed scoped claims:

```text
optimizer collecting metrics for <scope>, live_order_allowed=false
optimizer locally improving for <scope>, promotion blocked pending validators
optimizer robustly improving for <scope>, not LIVE_READY
optimizer stable candidate for <scope>, writer input not generated
optimizer writer-input eligible for <scope>, LIVE_READY snapshot not written
optimizer live burn-in observing for <scope>, scale-up blocked pending validators
```

### 0F.13 Required registry additions

MVP-0 or the first convergence-related contract patch must add or generate these registry groups or stricter equivalents.

```text
profit_convergence_state
profit_convergence_objective_component
profit_convergence_penalty_component
profit_convergence_memory_type
profit_convergence_root_cause
profit_convergence_controller_state
profit_convergence_action
profit_convergence_claim_type
risk_scaling_decision_type
risk_scaling_trigger
market_regime_adaptation_signal
failure_pattern_status
model_drift_status
survival_layer_action
convergence_blocker_code
```

Convergence blocker codes that affect trading, promotion, live readiness, or operator-facing readiness must be projected into no_trade_reason or live_blocker_code.

Required convergence blocker codes:

```text
CONVERGENCE_OBJECTIVE_MISSING
CONVERGENCE_MEMORY_MISSING
CONVERGENCE_MEMORY_STALE
CONVERGENCE_STATE_UNTESTED
CONVERGENCE_CLAIM_UNVERIFIED
FAILURE_ANALYSIS_MISSING
ROOT_CAUSE_UNKNOWN_LIVE_AFFECTING
MODEL_DRIFT_DETECTED
REGIME_ADAPTATION_UNTESTED
EXPLORATION_EXPLOITATION_TRANSITION_UNTESTED
RISK_SCALING_UNTESTED
RISK_SCALING_UP_FORBIDDEN
SURVIVAL_LAYER_BLOCKED
DRAWDOWN_FREEZE_ACTIVE
LIVE_BURN_IN_FEEDBACK_MISSING
SCALE_UP_VALIDATOR_MISSING
SCALE_UP_NOT_ELIGIBLE
```

### 0F.14 Required schemas

MVP-0 or the first convergence-related contract patch must add these schemas or stricter equivalents.

```text
contracts/schema/convergence_objective_profile.schema.json
contracts/schema/optimizer_memory_state.schema.json
contracts/schema/strategy_performance_memory.schema.json
contracts/schema/failure_analysis_report.schema.json
contracts/schema/market_regime_adaptation_report.schema.json
contracts/schema/exploration_exploitation_policy.schema.json
contracts/schema/risk_scaling_decision.schema.json

contracts/schema/model_drift_report.schema.json
contracts/schema/live_burn_in_feedback_report.schema.json
contracts/schema/profit_convergence_cycle_report.schema.json
```

All convergence schemas must use `https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema`, must set `additionalProperties=false` unless explicitly justified, and must use registry-backed closed enums for convergence state, root cause, controller state, risk-scaling decision, drift status, survival action, and blocker code.

### 0F.16 Required validators

Add these validators or stricter equivalents.

```text
convergence_objective_profile_validator
optimizer_memory_state_validator
strategy_performance_memory_validator
failure_analysis_validator
root_cause_classifier_validator
market_regime_adaptation_validator
exploration_exploitation_policy_validator
risk_scaling_decision_validator
convergence_assessment_validator
model_drift_validator
live_burn_in_feedback_validator
profit_convergence_cycle_validator
survival_layer_validator
scale_up_eligibility_validator
convergence_claim_validator_v2
```

Validator dependency rules:

```text
convergence_assessment_validator depends on convergence_objective_profile_validator, optimizer_memory_state_validator, strategy_performance_memory_validator, overfit_diagnostic_validator, execution_feedback_loop_validator, and model_drift_validator.
risk_scaling_decision_validator depends on live_burn_in_feedback_validator when scale-up is proposed, paper_live_parity_validator, execution_quality_measurement_validator, and survival_layer_validator.
exploration_exploitation_policy_validator depends on ranking_stability_validator, optimizer_resource_budget_validator, overfit_diagnostic_validator, and convergence_assessment_validator.
market_regime_adaptation_validator depends on symbol_strategy_regime_fit_validator, data_freshness_validator, and regime_scope_validator.
failure_analysis_validator depends on realized execution measurement when execution failure is claimed.
scale_up_eligibility_validator depends on risk_scaling_decision_validator, live_burn_in_feedback_validator, paper_live_parity_validator, and all live-blocking validators.
```

A parent validator cannot PASS when any required dependency is FAIL, BLOCKED, UNTESTED, STALE, or TIMEOUT.

### 0F.17 Required fixtures

Every convergence-related validator that can influence promotion, scale-up, live readiness, or operator-facing readiness requires PASS, FAIL, and BLOCKED fixtures.

Minimum fixture cases:

```text
raw PnL improves while net_ev_after_cost is negative -> FAIL
candidate improves in short window but fails OOS -> BLOCKED
candidate passes paper but live burn-in slippage diverges -> BLOCKED
candidate high score but failure root cause UNKNOWN_ROOT_CAUSE in live-affecting scope -> BLOCKED
regime shift occurs and strategy remains promoted without adaptation -> FAIL
risk scale-up proposed without burn-in evidence -> BLOCKED
risk scale-up proposed with drawdown breach -> BLOCKED
optimizer memory missing for repeated candidate -> BLOCKED
memory forgets failed candidate without audit -> FAIL
model drift detected but convergence claim remains ROBUSTLY_IMPROVING -> FAIL
scale-up eligible while emergency protection unavailable -> BLOCKED
survival layer blocked but optimizer score improves -> BLOCKED
```

### 0F.18 Dashboard display for convergence

Dashboard may display convergence status only as analysis status unless live readiness and live order allowed are independently true.

Required display fields:

```text
convergence_state
optimizer_status
optimizer_stage
optimizer_maturity_level
objective_score_band
primary_convergence_blocker_code
primary_convergence_blocker_message
last_convergence_assessment_id
last_memory_state_id
last_failure_analysis_id
last_risk_scaling_decision_id
live_order_ready
live_order_allowed
scale_up_eligible
```

Forbidden display wording:

```text
profit guaranteed
automatic profit
converged to profit
self-optimizing live
safe to scale automatically
ready to size up
```

Allowed wording:

```text
CONVERGENCE: COLLECTING, LIVE ORDERS BLOCKED
CONVERGENCE: LOCALLY IMPROVING, PROMOTION BLOCKED
CONVERGENCE: ROBUSTLY IMPROVING, NOT LIVE_READY
CONVERGENCE: WRITER INPUT ELIGIBLE, SNAPSHOT NOT WRITTEN
CONVERGENCE: LIVE BURN-IN OBSERVING, SCALE-UP BLOCKED
CONVERGENCE: SCALE-UP ELIGIBLE, OPERATOR AND VALIDATORS REQUIRED
```

### 0F.19 Patch result extension

Every convergence-related patch result must include these fields or stricter equivalents.

```text
profit_convergence_patch
convergence_layer_changed
convergence_state_before
convergence_state_after
objective_profile_changed
memory_schema_changed
memory_state_id
failure_analysis_required
failure_analysis_status
exploration_exploitation_policy_changed
regime_adaptation_changed
risk_scaling_policy_changed
survival_layer_changed
convergence_validators_required
convergence_validators_run
convergence_guardrail_result
convergence_live_mutation_detected
convergence_live_order_allowed_after
scale_up_eligibility_changed
scale_up_allowed_after
```

Required values:

```text
convergence_live_mutation_detected=false
convergence_live_order_allowed_after=false unless independent LIVE_ENABLING evidence exists
scale_up_allowed_after=false unless scale_up_eligibility_validator PASS and live_order_allowed remains true for exact scope
removed_requirements=[]
file_split=false
detail_reduction_allowed=false
```

### 0F.20 Final reinforced convergence operating rule

```text
The system may search aggressively, learn persistently, classify failures, adapt to regimes, calibrate execution, narrow parameters, and recommend conservative risk changes. It must never equate improvement with profit guarantee, never equate convergence with live permission, never mutate live config directly, never write LIVE_READY snapshot directly, never scale up without burn-in and validator evidence, and never weaken FinalDecision, risk veto, ledger, reconciliation, official API verification, emergency protection, live final guard, or survival rules.
```

# AUTOMATIC_PROFIT_CONVERGENCE_REINFORCEMENT_OVERLAY_END


# PROFITABILITY_CONVERGENCE_OPTIMIZER_OVERLAY_START

## 0E. Automatic profitability-convergence optimizer overlay

document status: active additive hardening supplement

This overlay adds an automatic profitability-convergence optimizer to the single-file TRADER_1 authority document. It does not delete, replace, shrink, summarize, move, or weaken any existing baseline content. Existing TRADER_1 content remains preserved below this overlay and remains available for detailed requirements, traceability, coverage, and implementation lookup.

```yaml
overlay_schema_id: trader1.profitability_convergence_optimizer_overlay.v1
patch_class: DOC_CONTRACT_PATCH
patch_date: "2026-04-28T06:49:25Z"
patch_method: additive_overlay_plus_verbatim_baseline_preservation
patch_input_trader1_file: "TRADER_1(128).md"
patch_input_trader1_sha256: "ac00634ffedd90ef195692e21454d86958601a71bb91a190ab84deeacaa1ab9d"
patch_input_agents_file: "AGENTS(136).md"
patch_input_agents_sha256: "d97e052ca0dd5dcd776baa93fbd92989e8a68d48bdef34c167060ae581d380b5"
file_split: false
removed_requirements: []
detail_reduction_allowed: false
semantic_reduction_allowed: false
retained_archive_preserved: true
baseline_body_preserved_verbatim: true
existing_detail_preserved: true
profit_guarantee_created: false
live_order_ready_after_document_patch: false
live_order_allowed_after_document_patch: false
can_live_trade_after_document_patch: false
```

### 0E.1 Purpose, non-goals, and safety invariant

The system must include an automatic profitability-convergence optimizer.

The optimizer's purpose is to increase the probability that REPLAY, PAPER, and SHADOW results converge toward higher net expected value after fees, spread, slippage, market impact, latency cost, funding cost when applicable, drawdown, tail-risk, concentration, regime instability, execution-quality, and overfit penalties.

The optimizer is not a profit guarantee.

```text
optimizer existence -> no profitability claim
optimizer improvement -> no profitability claim
optimizer winner -> no live permission
optimizer winner -> no LIVE_READY snapshot
optimizer winner -> no ACTIVE snapshot
optimizer recommendation -> no direct live config mutation
optimizer score -> evidence candidate only
```

The optimizer cannot weaken any existing live blocker, promotion threshold, validator requirement, namespace rule, order safety rule, ledger rule, or authority rule. If this overlay and any lower retained wording conflict, apply the stricter fail-closed rule.

The default state remains fixed.

```text
live_order_ready=false
live_order_allowed=false
can_live_trade=false
```

### 0E.2 Optimizer scope

The optimizer operates only inside evidence-producing modes unless a later LIVE_ENABLING evidence package explicitly permits exact-scope live burn-in.

Allowed default modes:

```text
REPLAY
PAPER
SHADOW
READ_ONLY for live-market observation only
```

Default forbidden actions:

```text
new live entry
live position add
live pyramiding
live risk profile upgrade
live parameter relaxation
live stop widening
LIVE_READY snapshot writing
ACTIVE snapshot mutation
live config mutation
```

Allowed outputs by default:

```text
OPTIMIZER_RESEARCH_SIGNAL
RANKED_EXPLORATION_CANDIDATE
REFINEMENT_CANDIDATE
PARAMETER_NARROWING_PROPOSAL
SYMBOL_STRATEGY_REGIME_FIT_REPORT
EXECUTION_FEEDBACK_ADJUSTMENT_PROPOSAL
LIVE_READY_CANDIDATE_WRITER_INPUT only after all existing promotion gates PASS
```

Forbidden interpretations:

```text
OPTIMIZER_RESEARCH_SIGNAL -> promotion evidence
RANKED_EXPLORATION_CANDIDATE -> LIVE_READY evidence
REFINEMENT_CANDIDATE -> LIVE_READY snapshot
PARAMETER_NARROWING_PROPOSAL -> live parameter bound
optimizer_top_rank -> live config
optimizer_score_improved -> profitability claim
optimizer_convergence_status -> live_order_allowed
```

### 0E.3 Optimizer maturity standard

The optimizer is sufficiently advanced only when it reaches maturity levels through evidence, not by code presence.

```text
Level 0: optimizer disabled or scaffold only
Level 1: metric collection exists for replay, paper, and shadow artifacts
Level 2: candidate scorecard and ranking exist with cost-adjusted objective
Level 3: constrained exploration and exploitation scheduler exists
Level 4: adaptive parameter narrowing exists with rollback and holdout protection
Level 5: symbol-strategy-regime fit learning exists
Level 6: execution feedback loop exists and updates future candidate scoring
Level 7: overfit diagnostics, robustness testing, and walk-forward/OOS validation exist
Level 8: LIVE_READY_CANDIDATE_WRITER_INPUT generation is possible only through writer gates
Level 9: limited live burn-in feedback may inform risk-reducing or conservative refinement only
```

Minimum useful level:

```text
MVP-2 -> Level 1 scaffold and metric collection
MVP-3 -> Level 3 ranking plus constrained exploration in PAPER/SHADOW
MVP-4 -> Level 6 read-only execution feedback and symbol/regime fit reporting
MVP-5 -> Level 8 writer input eligibility for exact-scope limited live only
```

A sufficient profitability-convergence system for this project requires at least Level 6 before claiming optimizer maturity in paper/shadow, and Level 8 before any optimizer output can be considered as input to LIVE_READY snapshot writing. Level 9 is required before the optimizer may influence live scale-up proposals. Even at Level 9, the optimizer must not directly mutate live configuration.
